topics.summary: chrome.runtime messaging and lifecycle events, the shared chrome.events machinery, and the alarms/idle/power scheduling APIs.
chrome.offscreen DOM access, chrome.gcm/instanceID cloud messaging, chrome.extension, and the chrome.types/extensionTypes/dom namespaces.
chrome.scripting (executeScript, insertCSS, registerContentScripts) and chrome.userScripts for arbitrary user code.
chrome.tabs, chrome.tabGroups, and chrome.windows for managing tabs, groups, and browser windows.
chrome.webNavigation, sessions, history, topSites, bookmarks, readingList, processes, and search.
chrome.declarativeNetRequest, webRequest, declarativeContent, proxy, cookies, and dns; MV3 blocking is declarative.
chrome.storage areas and quotas plus contentSettings, privacy, fontSettings, and browsingData.
chrome.action, contextMenus, sidePanel, omnibox, notifications, and commands.
chrome.devtools.inspectedWindow/network/panels/performance/recorder and chrome.debugger (CDP).
chrome.identity OAuth flows, loginState, certificateProvider, platformKeys, and webAuthenticationProxy.
chrome.tabCapture, desktopCapture, pageCapture, audio, tts, and ttsEngine.
chrome.downloads, chrome.management, and the runtime chrome.permissions API for optional permissions.
system.cpu/display/memory/storage, systemLog, printing, printerProvider, printingMetrics, documentScan, and accessibilityFeatures.
enterprise.deviceAttributes/hardwarePlatform/login/networkingAttributes/platformKeys, fileBrowserHandler, fileSystemProvider, input.ime, wallpaper, and vpnProvider.
chrome.i18n message lookup, UI language, language detection, default_locale, and _locales catalogs.
The MV3 package, manifest, service worker, content scripts, UI surfaces, and how they communicate.
Registering the service worker, its event-driven lifecycle, termination, top-level listeners, alarms, and offscreen documents.
Static vs dynamic injection, the ISOLATED vs MAIN world, run_at timing, match patterns, and CSS injection.
One-time messages, ports, cross-extension and external messaging, and native messaging with the nativeMessaging permission.
permissions vs host_permissions, optional permissions, runtime requests, activeTab, and host match patterns.
Choosing chrome.storage areas, persisting worker state, cookies access, and cross-origin isolation keys.
Install/update/startup events, update rollout, onUpdateAvailable/requestUpdateCheck, and setUninstallURL.
No remotely hosted code, content_security_policy, web_accessible_resources, and minimal-permission/privacy practices.
Concrete OLD-to-NEW manifest, API, background, and security changes to convert an MV2 extension to MV3.
The manifest file format and identity keys: manifest_version, name, version, icons, default_locale, key, and more.
Functional keys: action, background, content_scripts, permissions, content_security_policy, side_panel, oauth2, and others.
Niche keys: file_browser_handlers, file_handlers, file_system_provider_capabilities, input_components, tts_engine, COEP/COOP, shared_modules, trial_tokens, requirements, event_rules, devtools_page.
The named permission strings usable in permissions/optional_permissions, grouped by area, plus the host-permission distinction.
Accessibility, favicons via _favicon/, localization message formats, notifications, and extending DevTools.
Geolocation, screen capture, WebSockets, WebHID, WebUSB, and origin trials from an MV3 extension.
OAuth via chrome.identity, Google Analytics 4 Measurement Protocol, distribution, and loading an unpacked extension.
Inspecting the service worker/popup/content scripts, Jest unit tests, and Puppeteer/Selenium end-to-end testing.
